target.claim: Platform-equivalent full OS sandbox coverage for script workers
target.separation: This matrix is separate from Postman API parity and from scripted load-test support.

claimSurfaces.platform-os-sandbox: Native OS sandbox coverage and packaged validation across tier-one desktop platforms.
claimSurfaces.postman-api-parity: Postman script API behavior and imported-script observable output.
claimSurfaces.load-test-scripting: Future scripted load-test contract; currently out of scope.
claimSurfaces.release-gate: Validation plumbing required to keep the matrix current.

statuses.implemented: Implemented and covered by automated validation or source-owned contract checks.
statuses.blocked-native-backend: Blocked until a native OS sandbox backend and packaged validation exist for this platform.
statuses.deferred-decision: Security/product decision is intentionally deferred and blocks the stronger public claim.
statuses.out-of-scope: Explicitly outside the current claim surface.
statuses.validation-hook: Validation plumbing exists and must stay release-gated.

rows.target: Linux script workers launch through bubblewrap with unshared namespaces, no host network namespace, dropped capabilities, cleared environment, and private writable tmpfs mounts.
rows.securityDecision: Use bubblewrap as the first OS-level backend and require it for Linux production validation.

rows.target: Packaged Linux artifacts must prove the OS sandbox backend, Node permission flags, ASAR/path behavior, and sandbox worker launch still work after packaging.
rows.securityDecision: Keep packaged Linux OS-sandbox validation in CI and release workflows before shipping artifacts.

rows.target: Linux bubblewrap launches install a seccomp cBPF deny policy for high-risk kernel APIs such as bpf, ptrace, keyring, mount, process_vm_*, perf_event_open, io_uring, and nested namespace syscalls.
rows.securityDecision: Keep the dangerous-syscall deny policy in the Linux release gate while the stronger deny-by-default decision is tracked separately.

rows.target: Decide whether platform-equivalent "full syscall-policy sandbox" requires a maintained deny-by-default seccomp-BPF allowlist instead of the current dangerous-syscall deny policy.
rows.securityDecision: Do not claim platform-equivalent full OS sandbox coverage until this Linux syscall-policy standard is decided and implemented if required.
verificationRefs: NEXT_STEPS.MD Questions For Maintainer After Implementation

rows.target: Scripted load tests remain outside the Postman scripting parity claim unless a separate scripted-load-test contract is written and implemented.
rows.securityDecision: Do not execute request scripts during load tests in sandbox v1; avoid implying partial scripted-load compatibility.

rows.target: Packaged macOS artifacts must validate the native OS sandbox backend, Node permission flags, app bundle/ASAR path behavior, and script-worker launch behavior on a macOS runner.
rows.securityDecision: macOS release validation cannot satisfy the platform-equivalent OS sandbox claim until it proves the native backend in packaged artifacts.
verificationRefs: future npm run sandbox:validate:packaged on macos-latest with native OS backend assertions

rows.target: Implement a native macOS OS sandbox backend, such as seatbelt/sandbox-exec replacement strategy or an equivalent hardened helper, for script workers.
rows.securityDecision: Fail closed or downgrade the public claim on macOS until a native OS sandbox backend exists; do not silently rely on node:vm alone.
verificationRefs: future npm run sandbox:validate on macos-latest

rows.target: Postman script API parity is tracked by the Postman parity matrix and claim gate; platform OS sandbox completion is intentionally not part of npm run postman:parity:claim.
rows.securityDecision: Keep compatibility gaps and platform OS sandbox gaps on separate claim gates so one cannot mask the other.

rows.target: The OS-sandbox platform matrix is generated, committed, validated, and wired into check, CI, release workflow, and release-gate manifest validation.
rows.securityDecision: Require matrix freshness and structure in normal validation, while keeping the stronger platform-equivalent claim as a separate intentionally failing gate until blockers are resolved.

rows.target: Implement a native Windows OS sandbox backend, such as AppContainer or an equivalent restricted-token/job-object launch model, for script workers.
rows.securityDecision: Fail closed or downgrade the public claim on Windows until a native OS sandbox backend exists; do not silently rely on node:vm alone.
verificationRefs: future npm run sandbox:validate on windows-latest

rows.target: Packaged Windows artifacts must validate the native OS sandbox backend, Node permission flags, packaged path behavior, and script-worker launch behavior on a Windows runner.
rows.securityDecision: Windows release validation cannot satisfy the platform-equivalent OS sandbox claim until it proves the native backend in packaged artifacts.
verificationRefs: future npm run sandbox:validate:packaged on windows-latest with native OS backend assertions
